key_points: Parliament Hall nicknamed 'Far-liament' — 15-min walk to everything
No Greek housing on campus; all fraternities/sororities are off-site
Housing office accused of discrimination; sign-up required 10-11 months ahead
key_quotes: avoid this at all costs, you will be walking 15 minutes to get anywhere
There's a reason they call it 'Far-liament'
It's so lonely in Parliament. I completely agree, not for Freshman.

key_points: Commuter school culture means most students arrive, study, and leave fast
Making friends is hard unless you join clubs or live in dorms
Campus does have active wildlife photography fans, D&D, and niche communities
key_quotes: It's a commuter school. Everyone drives in, gets their stuff done and wants to get out as soon as possible.
I always found it hard to make friends if you're not in a club on campus.
Most (if not all) of my classmates were always dead silent during class

key_points: Soft-serve ice cream downgraded and universally hated by dining hall students
Subway closed with no replacement opened before school started
Microwaves removed campus-wide; finding one is now described as 'a side quest'
key_quotes: they changed the soft serve in the dining hall and i hate it. they replaced it with this watery bullshit.
Finding a microwave on campus is a whole side quest lmao
Why does FAU keep closing more campus restaurants but doesn't open any new places before school starts?

key_points: Free massage chairs available at CAPS center on 2nd floor of breezeway
New PhD and international students report significant loneliness and isolation
Academic failure and suspension posts show students feeling overwhelmed with little support
key_quotes: It has been two months since I arrived in Boca Raton. I feel lonely and dull all the time.
Take care of your mental health first (nobody ever says that)
I failed this semester and can't walk at graduation. My parents are expecting me to walk and they will kill me.

key_points: Transfer students report calling repeatedly with no answers — must go in person
Refund disbursements are delayed and amounts confuse students regularly
Financial aid office gives vague timelines; 'wait' is the standard response
key_quotes: I had to go to them physically to see if they even HAVE an offer. Calling them will result with the same answer of 'wait'.
I still haven't gotten my financial aid offer. I've called a million times with no clear answers except wait.
I just got a refund at the dead of night and I'm a little bit worried because the amount is not what I was expecting.

key_points: Some genuinely excellent, passionate professors — but quality varies wildly by department
Rampant ChatGPT cheating in discussion boards; students not embarrassed about it
WiFi unreliable during exams; Canvas crashed mid-exam with no warning
key_quotes: i just had to scroll through like 10 pages of the exact same paragraph because 150 students are using chatgpt
FAU always had these people. As someone whose recently graduated… it highly depends on your major.
I hate taking exams on campus because you never know if the WiFi will cut out.

key_points: Multiple professors placed on leave for social media posts — campus politically tense
Advisors range from dismissive to actively giving wrong advice
Portal and website crashes during high-traffic moments described as 'embarrassing'
key_quotes: Mine literally asked me if I ever wanted to graduate because I was struggling with a class.
One of the freshman advisors told me I didn't have to take general chemistry II my second semester. I'm a chemistry major.
Quite honestly embarrassing that a reputable university can't manage to keep their own website active.

key_points: Boca Raton location is a genuine perk — beach access, South Florida lifestyle
Parking is a serious first-week nightmare; construction removed lots without adding replacements
Campus wildlife is a quirky highlight: raccoons, iguanas, gopher tortoises, rare birds
key_quotes: Go to the beach and study. Come on. Students all around the country are laughing at your confusion.
This morning they closed off 2 entire parking lots to build a new building… but haven't provided alternative parking.
Today was my first day and I didn't find a parking space till 12:10… an hour of going from lot to lot is insane.

key_points: Career prep is highly major-dependent — accounting strong, CS disappointing
Accounting grads report smooth job placement right out of college
CS and some other majors feel underprepared and poorly connected to employers
key_quotes: As someone whose recently graduated… some degrees — such as accounting — you're good to go right out of college. Others like computer science *inhales*
Do you feel like FAU has done well preparing you for the next step? — 'no lmao'
Roughly what do you want to do when you graduate? Which companies hire in that field? Start with this in mind.

key_points: In-state tuition seen as a strong value, especially living at home to avoid debt
Out-of-state students pay significantly more with fewer obvious benefits over FIU
Community college then transfer to FAU widely recommended as the smartest financial path
key_quotes: Zero or very little debt is such a perk especially since I'm about to graduate.
It's a path to a 4 year university where at the end of the day, your degree will say FAU.
As an old lady who works in academia, I would advise anyone this is a great route. You save money, get your pre-reqs done in a less chaotic environment.

key_points: Laid-back, non-traditional commuter school with a warm South Florida feel
Strong sense of community among those who engage — clubs, dorms, niche groups
Scored 7.4 out of 10 in a multi-Florida-campus comparison video
key_quotes: I graduated FAU at 29. You're good. It was a commuter school with lots of nontraditional students.
definitely not! i feel like everyone here is around the age 21-30 so you'll find people similar in age
FAU has given me the greatest opportunity in the higher education field. I love, love, and love this paradise.

key_points: **Campus Safety:** Non-students repeatedly trespass to harass and secretly film female students
**Faculty Conduct:** Multiple professors made threatening or extremist social media posts while employed
**Slurs & Harassment:** Anti-LGBTQ and other slurs normalized in public campus spaces
key_quotes: He's been in a number of controversies. He assaulted and harassed students and illegally entered classrooms.
'Be very afraid. We are going to hunt you down.' — reported FAU finance professor's social media post
my friend begged him to take down her video and he wouldn't — on non-student secretly recording women

key_points: FAU's Florida Civic Literacy Exam prep videos are a campus-made resource used statewide
Wilkes Honors College is a small, tight-knit program with strong academic culture
Tortuga Trail nature area on campus is a quiet wildlife sanctuary most students miss
key_quotes: Literally the only thing about FAU that I miss — on Tortuga Trail
I'm a third year at Wilkes. Take things slowly and don't be afraid to experiment.
These FAU videos helped me pass my Florida civic literacy exam today — I don't even attend FAU.
